title: Fact-check: Russian plane from Syria via Erebuni airport and mercenary transfer claims
content: Fact-check: To transfer mercenaries from Latakia or another part of Syria, it is not necessary to do it through Armenia. The fact that the Russian RA-85041 plane arrived from Syria to the Armenian Erebuni airport, and from there flew to the Russian Federation, does not prove the fact of transferring mercenaries on that plane.

title: Hayantar Employee Salaries to Increase by 20-70,000 AMD
content: From March 2021, the salaries of employees of Hayantar SNCO will increase by 20-70,000 AMD, including salaries of forest rangers and forest managers increasing by 20,000 AMD. The decision was made possible through cost optimization and savings from the Ministry of Environment.

title: The \"Fair Armenia\" party issues an official statement on parliamentary elections
content:
On June 27, 2021, the Central Electoral Commission of Armenia summed up the results of the snap parliamentary elections and made a decision on forming a National Assembly, and distributing the mandates. The "Fair Armenia" party is only 4 months old, but the registered 4000 votes show that a certain positive attitude has been formed towards the party.

2. **[Azerbaijan intends to close the Lachin Corridor - Alen Simonyan](https://infocom.am/en/article/93431)** — On December 5, RA National Assembly Speaker Alen Simonyan gave a speech at the joint session of the Council of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Collective Security Treaty Organization and at the 15th plenary session.

3. **[Daily News Digest [29.10.2021]](https://infocom.am/en/Article/67228)** — The parliamentary faction "I have Honor" confirms that they will meet with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan only on one agenda - on the issue of his resignation. Also covered PACE co-rapporteurs' visit to Armenia and other parliamentary developments.

4. **[Financial means of MP candidates: "Prosperous Armenia" party](https://infocom.am/en/Article/57154)** — The property, income, and interests declarations of the "Prosperous Armenia" party candidates were presented. Gagik Tsarukyan is the PM candidate of the "Prosperous Armenia" party and has declared the largest amount of money.

5. **["Armenia" alliance](https://infocom.am/en/Infotags/infotag?i=879)** — The "Armenia" (Hayastan) alliance was founded in 2021 to participate in the snap parliamentary elections, created by Robert Kocharyan, the "Armenian Revolutionary Federation" and the "Reviving Armenia" parties, with Kocharyan heading the electoral list.

6. **[Peace agenda has broad public consensus, says MP Lena Nazaryan](https://infocom.am/en/Article/80627)** — Member of Parliament from the ruling Civil Contract bloc Lena Nazaryan says the peace agenda has a "very broad public consensus," speaking in parliament about consensus while meeting with citizens and families of fallen troops.

7. **[Infocom news page (March 29, 2022)](https://infocom.am/en/news?date=2022-03-29)** — Featured headlines including "A debate between Alen Simonyan and the head of the Azerbaijani Parliament" and other regional security developments.

8. **[Aram Vardevanyan on violations registered by the "Armenia" alliance](https://infocom.am/en/Article/58133)** — "For the first time in the whole period of the electoral process in Armenia the circumstance of abuse of administrative resources by the ruling force was registered as a legal fact by a decision made by the Administrative Court."

9. **[#armvote2021 Daily News Digest [05.06.2021]](https://infocom.am/en/Article/56538)** — According to polls conducted from May 31 to June 5, 22.4% of respondents favored the "Civil Contract" party, 20.6% the "Armenia" alliance, 4.2% "Prosperous Armenia" party, and 3.9% the "I Have Honor" alliance.

These results are from **infocom.am**, an Armenian news outlet covering parliamentary elections, political alliances, and legislative affairs in Armenia.

title: There is no discussion or agreement on the redemarcation of the Armenian-Turkish border
content:
There is no discussion or agreement on the redemarcation of the Armenian-Turkish border, according to Vahan Hunanyan, the spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia.

2. **[The delegation of the EU technical assessment mission was in the border settlements of Vardenis](https://infocom.am/en/article/90384)** — On October 18, the delegation of the EU technical assessment mission, along with military and community leaders, visited the border zones of Tretuk and Kutakan settlements of the Vardenis community, which were affected by the Azerbaijani aggression of 2022.

3. **[Iran will not tolerate border changes in the region](https://infocom.am/en/article/89036)** — The Chief of the General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces emphasized that Iran will not tolerate border changes in the region.

4. **[The territorial integrity of Azerbaijan has nothing to do with NKR and its status - Artsakh MFA statement](https://infocom.am/en/article/93320)** — The settlement of relations between Armenia and Azerbaijan, including the settlement of the border issue between the two countries, cannot and should not be related to the status of the Republic of Artsakh.

5. **[Peace agenda has broad public consensus, says MP Lena Nazaryan](https://infocom.am/en/Article/80627)** — This article discusses public debate around border security and the peace agenda between Armenia and Azerbaijan.

6. **[ԱՄdelays Archives - Infocom](https://infocom.am/infotag/306/)** — An Armenian-language page about the United States, including geographic information about its borders.

These results are all from **infocom.am**, an Armenian news aggregation site, and primarily cover topics related to Armenian border issues with Azerbaijan, Turkey, and regional geopolitics.

title: The construction of 9 houses almost completed in Shurnukh
content: The Chairman of the Urban Development Committee, Armen Ghularyan, and the Head of the Urban Planning Implementation Department, Arsen Mirzoyan, paid a working visit to the Syunik region to supervise the construction of a new district in the Shurnukh settlement. The construction works of 9 houses in the district, including 13 residential houses, are almost completed, and 4 are in progress. A new road in the district was built, and the construction works of engineering infrastructures and fences were started. The construction works of the new district in the Shurnukh settlement of the expanded community of Goris started on April 26, 2021, with the project costing around 1.2 billion AMD.

title: infocom.am/en/article/89661
content:
An article discussing Russia's geopolitical interests in the region. Key points include:
   - Russia is described as "the main beneficiary of the corridor through the territory of Armenia, because both Azerbaijan, Turkey, and Russia want a corridor through the territory of Armenia under the control of the Russian Federation."
   - One key reason for Russia's shift was "the growing role of Turkey and the importance for Russia of Turkey's more or less balanced position during Russia's irreversible conflict with the West."
   - The article claims Russia wants Armenia to become "part of the 'Russia-Belarus' union state or Russia."

2. **[Article 87185](https://infocom.am/en/article/87185)** – An interview with David Babayan about the Artsakh peace agreement. Babayan argues that recognizing Artsakh as part of Azerbaijan "will be a catastrophe, this will be a death sentence for the Republic of Armenia as a state." He references Turkey historically, noting that "Turkey in a difficult situation in 1920-21" and "Azerbaijan in a difficult situation in 1994" both managed to overcome their challenges.

3. **[Infocom.am homepage](https://infocom.am/)** – The main page of the site, which is an Armenian-language independent media outlet.

4. Additional pages included tag/filter pages from the site with limited content.

title: The territorial integrity of Azerbaijan has nothing to do with NKR and its status - Artsakh MFA statement
content:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Artsakh announced that the official position of the people and authorities of Artsakh remains unchanged: Artsakh will not be part of Azerbaijan.

2. **[Dispute in Getavan](https://infocom.am/en/article/93387)** — A convoy with Azerbaijanis in civilian clothes stopped in the Getavan community of the Martakert region, after which they behaved rudely and provocatively towards Armenian youngsters, leading to a dispute and violence.

3. **[David Babayan Interview](https://infocom.am/en/article/87185)** — Artsakh Foreign Minister Davit Babayan stated that Artsakh cannot be the price of a peace treaty. He emphasized that the principle should be that Artsakh is not a part of Azerbaijan.

4. **[Almaty Declaration and Artsakh Status](https://infocom.am/en/article/93329)** — This article discusses how referring to the Almaty declaration does not preclude opportunities to address the status of Artsakh.

5. **[Human Rights in Hadrut](https://infocom.am/en/Article/66259)** — The Human Rights Defender of Artsakh, Gegham Stepanyan, described occupied Hadrut as undeniable proof of Azerbaijan's policy of hatred and ethnic cleansing against Armenians.

6. **[Defense Minister Appointment](https://infocom.am/en/Article/64817)** — President Arayik Harutyunyan signed a decree appointing Kamo Vardanyan as the Minister of Defense of the Republic of Artsakh.

7. **[Martial Law Decree](https://infocom.am/en/Article/78705)** — Coverage of President Harutyunyan signing a decree on temporary restrictions on rights and freedoms under martial law, with responsibility for enforcement resting on the Artsakh Republic Ministry of Internal Affairs and the National Security Service.

8. **[Aspram Krpeyan to Become MP](https://infocom.am/en/Article/67446)** — Aspram Krpeyan, the daughter of the national hero of Artsakh Tatul Krpeyan, was set to become an MP from the "Armenia" (Hayastan) alliance.

9. **[Aghavno and Berdzor Resettlement](https://infocom.am/en/Article/86600)** — Residents of Berdzor, Aghavno, and Sus villages of Artsakh's Kashatagh region had until August 25 to leave their settlements as they came under Azerbaijan's control, and residents were taking out their last belongings.

10. **[March 29, 2022 News](https://infocom.am/en/news?date=2022-03-29)** — No significant ceasefire violations were registered along the Artsakh line of contact, though the situation remained tense, with Azerbaijani troops remaining at bases in Karaglukh adjacent to Parukh.

title: Azerbaijan intends to close the Lachin Corridor - Alen Simonyan
content: Coverage of the Azerbaijani blockade of the Lachin Corridor. The 'Regional Development' NGO linked to the Azerbaijani government was behind the so-called environmental protest blocking the corridor. The European Parliament adopted a resolution deploring the severe humanitarian crisis caused by the blockade and condemning the inactivity of Russian peacekeepers.

author: infocom.am

title: Pashinyan on Russian peacekeepers
content:
Pashinyan stated that Armenia is a firm supporter of the presence of Russian peacekeeping troops in Nagorno-Karabakh, but it is unacceptable for them to become "a silent witness to the depopulation of the Nagorno-Karabakh region." He emphasized that the closure of the Lachin Corridor is a gross violation of point 6 of the tripartite statement of November 9, 2020.

2. **[Almaty Declaration and the status of Artsakh](https://infocom.am/en/article/93329)** – Eduard Aghajanyan, Chairman of the Standing Committee on Foreign Relations, addressed Lavrov's statement regarding the Almaty Declaration and the question of the status of Nagorno-Karabakh. He stated that by referring to the Almaty declaration, no opportunity was lost, and it does not mean that there will not be an opportunity to address the status in the future.

3. **[Daily News Digest – October 29, 2021](https://infocom.am/en/Article/67228)** – PACE co-rapporteurs planned a fact-finding visit to Armenia in November 2021, with discussions focusing on electoral reform, judicial independence, the media situation, and the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and its consequences on democratic institutions in Armenia.

4. **[Alen Simonyan on Azerbaijan and Lachin Corridor](https://infocom.am/en/article/93431)** – Speaker Alen Simonyan stated that Armenia's task is to protect the population of Nagorno-Karabakh, noting that Azerbaijan had started announcing that the Nagorno-Karabakh problem no longer exists and demanded Russian peacekeepers stop using the name "Nagorno-Karabakh" and Armenian place names. He also warned of Azerbaijan's intention to close the Lachin Corridor, calling it "the only full-fledged road of humanitarian significance for the livelihood of the Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh."

5. **[Armenia-Russia relations discussion](https://infocom.am/en/article/84439)** – A sharp decline in Russian soft power polling in Armenia was attributed to the change in security environment and the defeat in the Second Karabakh War. Both Armenia and Azerbaijan viewed Moscow as the most important mediator or decision-maker in the Karabakh conflict.

These articles span from 2021 to 2024 and cover various aspects of the Nagorno-Karabakh situation as reported by Infocom.am.

title: There will be some actions, there will be answers - Zakharova announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission
content: Maria Zakharova, representative of the Russian Foreign Ministry, announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission in the border regions of Armenia. RA President Vahagn Khachaturyan welcomed the deployment of the EU's long-term 100-person monitoring mission along the Armenian border with Azerbaijan.

title: Vahram Dumanyan appointed as an adviser to the President of Armenia
content: Vahram Dumanyan, former Minister of Education, Science, Culture and Sports of the Republic of Armenia, was appointed adviser to the President of Armenia. He had submitted an application for dismissal from the position of minister on December 12, 2022. He was appointed to the position on November 23, 2020, and before that held the position of dean of the Faculty of Informatics and Applied Mathematics of YSU. Zhanna Andreasyan was appointed the new minister on December 13, 2022.
excerpt: Vahram Dumanyan, former Minister of Education, Science, Culture and Sports of the Republic of Armenia, was appointed adviser to the President of Armenia. He had submitted an application for dismissal

title: Ararat Mirzoyan met with Jordanian Foreign Minister Ayman al-Ṣafadī
content:
RA Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan met with Ayman al-Ṣafadī, Deputy Prime Minister of the Hashemites Kingdom of Jordan, Minister of Foreign Affairs and Diaspora Affairs, in Cairo on March 8.

2. **[Snap parliamentary elections 2021: election campaign day 4](https://infocom.am/56848/)** – The article discusses changing laws referring to the Diaspora to give an opportunity to have MPs elected by Diaspora in the National Assembly. The Pan-Armenian National Statehood Party stated that "if we have a huge Diaspora, we think that Diaspora should have its involvement in the state-building of our country."

3. **[David Babayan claims the peace agreement cannot be at the cost of Artsakh](https://infocom.am/en/article/87185)** – Babayan warned that recognizing Artsakh as part of Azerbaijan "will break the unity of the Armenians, which is already undermined: Diaspora, Artsakh, mother Armenia."

4. **[Infocom (new.infocom.am)](https://new.infocom.am/3413/)** – Limited description available for this page.

title: Infocom (Russian version)
content:

title: Animal trade: the uncertain destiny of animals imported into Armenia and the tacit consent of the state
content: Between 2017 and 2022, nearly 4,000 wild animals were imported into Armenia from various parts of the world. The Ministry of Environment and other authorities lack comprehensive information about imported and exported animals. In 2021, 45 giraffes were imported into Armenia; one perished during transfer and another died shortly after arriving, with the fate of the remaining giraffes currently unknown.

title: Suspect Donations and Unidentified Sources of the Ruling Civil Contract's Election Campaign Finances
content: Investigation into suspicious transactions associated with the Civil Contract party's 2023 Yerevan Council of Elders election campaign. A citizen's ID card and signature were exploited to channel funds of unverified origin into the party's account. The party did not disclose the identities of 987 donors who contributed 506,532,950 AMD despite repeated media requests. Infocom examined and identified nearly all individuals who contributed donations exceeding 1 million AMD.
excerpt: Investigation into suspicious transactions associated with the Civil Contract party's 2023 Yerevan Council of Elders election campaign. A citizen's ID card and signature were exploited to channel fund
